title: More Nonsense Limerick 27
content:
There was a young lady in white,
    Who looked out at the depths of the night;
    But the birds of the air, 
    Filled her heart with despair,
    And oppressed that young lady in white.
meaning:
Edward Lear's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "More Nonsense Limerick 27"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
